# Implementation Readiness Checklist: Crosslog MVP Phase 1

**Purpose**: Validate that Phase 1 setup requirements are clear, complete, and traceable before implementation starts.
**Created**: 2026-06-15
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are Phase 1 workspace scope requirements fully defined for Web shell, Desktop shell, shared packages, tooling, scripts, test harnesses, and icon pipeline? [Completeness, Plan §Phase 3.1, Tasks §Phase 1]
- [x] CHK002 Are package boundary requirements documented for `packages/core`, `packages/platform`, `packages/ui`, `apps/web`, and `apps/desktop` before scaffold tasks begin? [Completeness, Spec §CR-003, Plan §Project Structure, Tasks §T006-T013]
- [x] CHK003 Are canonical package command requirements complete enough to cover lint, unit tests, integration tests, Web UI tests, Desktop UI tests, benchmarks, and Rust adapter tests? [Completeness, Plan §Canonical Package Commands, Tasks §T002]
- [x] CHK004 Are empty-shell requirements defined for both Web and Desktop so Phase 1 smoke tests have a requirements source? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008, Plan §Phase 3.1, Tasks §T007/T019/T028]
- [x] CHK005 Are icon pipeline requirements documented from source SVG through expected generated asset documentation without implying final release packaging is complete in Phase 1? [Completeness, Plan §Phase 3.1, Tasks §T027/T202]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is "all scripts exist, run from clean checkout" defined with clear working-directory, shell, and failure-exit expectations? [Clarity, Plan §Phase 3.1, Contracts §Build and Test Scripts]
- [x] CHK007 Is the distinction between local macOS phase validation and later Windows/Linux release readiness unambiguous? [Clarity, Plan §Quality Gates, Tasks §T029/T204-T212]
- [x] CHK008 Are WebdriverIO/tauri-driver and macOS XCTest/Accessibility responsibilities clearly separated for Desktop UI automation requirements? [Clarity, Spec §CR-006, Plan §Testing, Tasks §T016-T019]
- [x] CHK009 Are "workspace imports" and "setup smoke test" requirements specific enough to define which package boundaries must be importable? [Ambiguity, Tasks §T028, Plan §Shared Codebase Strategy]
- [x] CHK010 Is Phase 1 benchmark fixture generator scope clear enough to avoid implementing later performance benchmark behavior prematurely? [Clarity, Plan §Phase 3.1, Tasks §T026, Plan §Phase 3.8]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Are Phase 1 tasks consistent with the shared-codebase rule that business logic belongs in shared packages and app shells remain thin? [Consistency, Spec §CR-003, Plan §Architecture Design, Tasks §T006-T013]
- [x] CHK012 Are OS script requirements consistent between plan script matrix, script contracts, and Phase 1 task paths? [Consistency, Plan §Script Matrix by OS, Contracts §Build and Test Scripts, Tasks §T020-T025]
- [x] CHK013 Are Desktop setup requirements consistent between TypeScript package scaffold, Rust manifest, Tauri configuration, and Desktop UI harness tasks? [Consistency, Plan §Technical Context, Tasks §T008-T010/T016-T019]
- [x] CHK014 Are formatting and linting requirements aligned with TypeScript workspace setup rather than platform-specific app-only validation? [Consistency, Plan §Canonical Package Commands, Tasks §T003-T005/T011-T013]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Can Phase 1 completion be objectively measured from documented criteria rather than inferred from task completion alone? [Measurability, Plan §Phase 3.1, Plan §Quality Gates]
- [x] CHK016 Are script smoke requirements measurable for success and failure without requiring full future user story behavior? [Measurability, Contracts §Build and Test Scripts, Tasks §T020-T025/T029]
- [x] CHK017 Are empty-state smoke UI expectations measurable without depending on file loading, pane management, or synchronization behavior from later phases? [Measurability, Spec §FR-008, Plan §Phase 3.1, Tasks §T019/T028]
- [x] CHK018 Are Phase 1 validation commands traceable to exact scripts and package commands that should exist after setup? [Traceability, Plan §Canonical Package Commands, Tasks §T002/T023/T029]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK019 Are requirements defined for fresh checkout setup on each target OS before any user story implementation exists? [Coverage, Plan §Phase 3.1, Plan §Script Matrix by OS]
- [x] CHK020 Are requirements defined for both browser empty-state validation and Desktop empty-state validation in Phase 1? [Coverage, Spec §FR-008, Plan §Phase 3.1, Tasks §T015-T019]
- [x] CHK021 Are requirements defined for CI-compatible command names and OS wrapper scripts as separate but aligned surfaces? [Coverage, Plan §Phase 3.1, Plan §Canonical Package Commands, Tasks §T002/T020-T025]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK022 Are requirements defined for missing platform prerequisites during Phase 1 script execution, including whether failure messaging belongs in scripts or documentation? [Gap, Quickstart §Prerequisites, Contracts §Build and Test Scripts]
- [x] CHK023 Are requirements defined for environments where macOS XCTest/Accessibility prerequisites are unavailable during local setup validation? [Gap, Quickstart §Prerequisites, Plan §Testing, Tasks §T017-T019]
- [x] CHK024 Are requirements defined for how generated icon assets should be documented when binary icon generation is deferred or partially supported by local tooling? [Gap, Plan §Phase 3.1, Tasks §T027]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK025 Are Phase 1 automation requirements sufficient to preserve later read-only and inert-content security gates without implementing those behaviors early? [Security, Spec §CR-001-CR-002, Plan §Quality Gates, Tasks §T028-T029]
- [x] CHK026 Are Phase 1 performance-related requirements limited to fixture generation and benchmark command wiring, with later benchmark thresholds kept in later phases? [Performance, Spec §CR-005, Plan §Phase 3.1/3.8, Tasks §T026/T197-T200]
- [x] CHK027 Are accessibility-related setup requirements for macOS UI automation documented separately from product accessibility behavior requirements? [Coverage, Spec §CR-006, Plan §Testing, Tasks §T017-T019]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK028 Are Node.js, pnpm, Rust, Tauri, Playwright, WebdriverIO, tauri-driver, and Xcode prerequisite assumptions documented well enough for Phase 1 setup decisions? [Assumption, Quickstart §Prerequisites, Plan §Technical Context]
- [x] CHK029 Are dependency choices for React, Vite, Tauri 2, Vitest, Playwright, WebdriverIO, and TanStack-related workspace setup traceable to requirements rather than preference? [Dependency, Plan §Selected Stack Recommendation, Plan §Alternatives Considered]
- [x] CHK030 Are publication and package-distribution assumptions documented sufficiently to decide whether npm packaging metadata is required in Phase 1 manifests? [Assumption, Tasks §T002/T006/T008/T011-T013]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK031 Is the expected relationship between `pnpm test:ui:desktop` and OS-specific Desktop UI backends clear enough to prevent conflicting script behavior? [Ambiguity, Plan §Canonical Package Commands, Plan §Script Matrix by OS]
- [x] CHK032 Is the required minimum content of the Web and Desktop app shells defined enough to avoid implementing future pane behavior during setup? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-008, Plan §Phase 3.1, Tasks §T007/T019]
- [x] CHK033 Are Phase 1 task dependencies clear enough to distinguish sequential workspace bootstrap tasks from parallel configuration and script tasks? [Ambiguity, Tasks §Phase 1, Tasks §Parallel Opportunities]

## Notes

- Focus areas: Phase 1 workspace setup readiness, script matrix traceability, empty-shell smoke coverage, Desktop UI harness setup, icon pipeline, and package command readiness.
- Depth: Standard PR review.
- Actor/timing: Reviewer before Phase 1 implementation.
